chapter_title.en: Chapter 59 – Acceptance of a debt with an inheritor

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/253)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/253)
text: رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ أَقَرَّ بَعْضُ وَرَثَتِهِ لِرَجُلٍ بِدَيْنٍ قَالَ يَلْزَمُهُ ذَلِكَ فِي حِصَّتِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, and Husayn Bin Usman, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who died and one of his inheritors accepted a debt for a man. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is necessary (to be paid off) from their shares’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Muhammad ibn Hamzah and Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who dies and certain ones of his heirs confirm his indebtedness to a man, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It becomes binding upon them on their shares.’”
